perturbation.original_text: Each party will use the Confidential Information it receives solely for the purpose of evaluating the Acquisition and not for any other purpose and, except to the extent permitted by paragraph 1.5 hereof, will keep such Confidential Information strictly confidential, provided, however, that Confidential Information may be disclosed to such Representatives as needed to know such information for the purpose of evaluating and negotiating the terms of the Acquisition and for no other purpose.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party will use the Confidential Information it receives solely for the purpose of evaluating the Acquisition and also for general market research purposes and, except to the extent permitted by paragraph 1.5 hereof, will keep such Confidential Information confidential to the best of its ability, provided, however, that Confidential Information may be disclosed to such Representatives as needed to know such information for any business purpose of the Receiving Party.
perturbation.explanation: The original text restricts the use of Confidential Information solely for evaluating the Acquisition. The modified text allows its use for general market research. This is a direct contradiction because the original clause explicitly prohibits using the information for "any other purpose." Additionally, the original text states that Confidential Information may only be disclosed to Representatives for evaluating and negotiating the terms of the Acquisition, and for no other purpose. The modified text now states that it can be disclosed for any business purpose, contradicting the original limitation. It will creates conflict to Article 1.3.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party will use the Confidential Information it receives solely for the purpose of evaluating the Acquisition and not for any other purpose and, except to the extent permitted by paragraph 1.5 hereof, will keep such Confidential Information strictly confidential, provided, however, that Confidential Information may be disclosed to such Representatives as needed to know such information for the purpose of evaluating and negotiating the terms of the Acquisition and for no other purpose.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. The original text and the contradicted text state that Confidential Information can ONLY be used for evaluating the Acquisition. The changed text expands this use to include 'general market research purposes' and allows disclosure to Representatives for 'any business purpose,' directly conflicting with the original limitation in both the modified text and the contradicted text. This creates a strong in-text contradiction because the explicit restriction on use is removed, and the purpose of use is expanded, violating the structural flaw definition of a in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: In the event that the parties hereto do not proceed with the Acquisition by October 31, 2007, and, in any event, within five (5) days after being so requested by either party, both parties shall return or destroy all documents furnished by the other. Any oral Confidential Information shall remain subject to the confidentiality obligations set forth in this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event that the parties hereto do not proceed with the Acquisition by October 31, 2007, both parties may retain all documents furnished by the other indefinitely. Any oral Confidential Information shall no longer be subject to any confidentiality obligations set forth in this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates the return or destruction of documents if the Acquisition doesn't proceed, and maintains confidentiality for oral information. The modified text allows indefinite retention of documents and removes confidentiality for oral information. This directly contradicts the original clause and the spirit of Article 1, creating uncertainty around data handling obligations. Specifically, the modified section directly contradicts Article 1.5 by stating that any oral Confidential Information shall no longer be subject to any confidentiality obligations set forth in this Agreement, which means that both parties won't have to return or destroy all documents furnished by the other, which creates a contradiction with Article 1.5 of Property Rights Maintained.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event that the parties hereto do not proceed with the Acquisition by October 31, 2007, and, in any event, within five (5) days after being so requested by either party, both parties shall return or destroy all documents furnished by the other. Any oral Confidential Information shall remain subject to the confidentiality obligations set forth in this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text. The original text and contradicted text require the return or destruction of documents if the Acquisition doesn't proceed and maintains confidentiality for oral information. The modified text allows indefinite retention of documents and removes confidentiality for oral information. This creates a direct conflict in obligations regarding the handling of confidential information and documents, making the two sections incompatible. The changed text removes the requirement to destroy documents and revokes confidentiality of oral information which clearly and strongly contradicts the same requirement in the contradicted text, and introduces a structural flaw.

perturbation.original_text: Zulu and its principal shareholders agree that until October 31, 2007, or such shorter period if either party notifies the other that it no longer wishes to proceed with the Acquisition (“Term of this Agreement”), that they will not, directly or indirectly, solicit, initiate or encourage submission of proposals of offers from any third party relating to any acquisition, purchase or option to purchase an equity interest in Zulu, or any merger, consolidation or business combination with Zulu or the sale of substantially all of the assets of Zulu.
perturbation.changed_text: Zulu and its principal shareholders agree that, notwithstanding the ongoing discussions regarding the Acquisition, they are free to solicit, initiate, or encourage submission of proposals or offers from any third party relating to any acquisition, purchase or option to purchase an equity interest in Zulu, or any merger, consolidation, or business combination with Zulu, or the sale of substantially all of the assets of Zulu. This freedom applies even before October 31, 2007.
perturbation.explanation: The original standstill agreement prevents Zulu from soliciting other offers. The modified text explicitly allows Zulu to solicit other offers, creating a direct contradiction and undermining the purpose of the standstill agreement. This inserted perturbation will be contradictory to Article 2, since the original texts prohibits the parties to sollicit any proposal but in the modified text, it is explicitly allowed. The modified text now allows actions explicitly prohibited earlier.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Zulu and its principal shareholders agree that until October 31, 2007, or such shorter period if either party notifies the other that it no longer wishes to proceed with the Acquisition (“Term of this Agreement”), that they will not, directly or indirectly, solicit, initiate or encourage submission of proposals of offers from any third party relating to any acquisition, purchase or option to purchase an equity interest in Zulu, or any merger, consolidation or business combination with Zulu or the sale of substantially all of the assets of Zulu.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text (which is also the original text). The original text prevents Zulu from soliciting offers, while the changed text explicitly allows it, creating a direct conflict. The explanation provided further confirms this in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The parties hereto agree that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement and that in addition to other remedies, each offended party shall be entitled to specific performance and injunctive or other equitable relief, and in such circumstances the offending party agrees to waive posting of a bond to secure any such equitable relief hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: The parties hereto agree that money damages would be the sole and exclusive remedy for any breach of this Agreement, and that neither party shall be entitled to specific performance, injunctive, or other equitable relief under any circumstances. The offended party must post a bond to secure any monetary relief hereunder.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that money damages are insufficient and allows for equitable relief (injunctions, specific performance) without a bond. The modified text makes money damages the *only* remedy, prohibits equitable relief, and *requires* a bond. This is a complete reversal and contradicts the entire purpose of Article 4, regarding injunctions and specific performance. The modified text denies any of the remedies specified in the original Article 4, it creates a direct contradiction to the text of the original article, which stated that money damages were not sufficient
perturbation.contradicted_text: The parties hereto agree that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement and that in addition to other remedies, each offended party shall be entitled to specific performance and injunctive or other equitable relief, and in such circumstances the offending party agrees to waive posting of a bond to secure any such equitable relief hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text. The original clause states that money damages are insufficient and that equitable relief is available without a bond. The modified clause states that money damages are the *sole* remedy, equitable relief is prohibited, and a bond *is* required. This is a direct reversal, creating a strong contradiction. The explanation says the modified text denies any of the remedies specified in the original Article 4, it creates a direct contradiction to the text of the original article, which stated that money damages were not sufficient. This aligns with the Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: If a definitive agreement has not been entered into by such date, then this Agreement will have no further force or effect except for Articles 1, 3, 4, 5 and 6 hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: If a definitive agreement has not been entered into by such date, then this Agreement will have no further force or effect, including Articles 1, 3, 4, 5 and 6 hereof. From that moment, the parties won't be bound by the rules stipulated in the agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that even if a definitive agreement isn't reached, Articles 1, 3, 4, 5, and 6 remain in effect. The modified text eliminates this exception, causing the entire agreement to terminate, including the confidentiality provisions in Article 1, the non-circumvention in Article 3 and the Injunction in Article 4. This is contradictory. The original text maintains some key obligations even without a final deal. The modified text removes all obligations from the parties.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The parties hereto will use their best efforts to enter into a definitive acquisition agreement and any other documents that may be necessary in order to consummate the Acquisition by October 31, 2007. If a definitive agreement has not been entered into by such date, then this Agreement will have no further force or effect except for Articles 1, 3, 4, 5 and 6 hereof.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that even if a definitive agreement is not reached, Articles 1, 3, 4, 5, and 6 remain in effect. The changed text removes this exception, stating that the entire agreement will have no further force or effect, including those articles. This directly contradicts the original provision that certain articles survive termination. The contradicted text is the same as the original text, making the contradiction clear.
